nodes.comment: Black to live in 1 move. I thought the bent five formed by one move could save black, but I was wrong. This situation requires a more aggressive move that puts white in atari while forming two eyes.
This is not the solution in the book, but I can't find any way for white to kill this group. This is a bent five in a corner.
This is a mistake for white. The critical point is now between the two white stones. Black will play there, forming two eyes and putting both of white's dead stones in atari.
Black forms two eyes and white's stones are dead.

White sealed black's fate here by taking the key point in the bent five. Black cannot put white in atari without turning the bent five into a bent four. Black can place a stone in atari, but white will capture.
Black thinks they've formed two eyes, but they've put themselves in atari. White will capture, putting all of its blacks stones in atari. White cannot recapture becaue of ko.
I didn't realize that black's corner piece is in atari. See variation.
Big mistake for black, forming a straight four in gote with both key points taken.
Black killed itself by forming a bent four in gote.
Black puts white in atari and gets two eyes. This is the solution in the book.
Black to live in 1 move. Don't let the prospect of a straight three fool you.

I thought this was the right move, black has formed one eye and seems guaranteed to get a second. However, black's two groups are disconnected in gote and white will either capture the left group or force black to connect them in a way that leaves no room for a second eye.

This is the smart move for white. Black has to capture in a way that leaves the capturing stone disconnected and surrounded. Black will inveitably lose more stones or reinforce to the point they can't form a second eye.
This is a mistake for white that lets black live. They should invade black's big eye, taking advantage of the unconnected stone instead of encouraging its defense.
This is the correct move for white that seals black's fate.
Big mistake for white. Black gets to form his second eye and capture white's stone.

This is the move that guarantees black's life, as given in the book. It would be an illegal move for white to take the critical (middle) point in the straight three. If black's groups had been surrounded from the outside, this would be a very different situation.
Black to live in one move. This was trickier than I thought.
This is a mistake as black is forming the pyramid four in gote.
This is a mistake as black is forming the bulky five in gote.
This looks promising, but it's not connected to the rest of the group, so it ends up being either bulky five or pyramid four in gote.

This is the only move that will guarantee can live. It immediately forms one eye and puts the invading white stone in atari. White can only ignore the atari, giving black its second eye, or run out in vain, giving black even more points and territory in the second eye.
Black to live in 1 move.
This is the move that saves black. Puts white in atari with plenty of room for two eyes. Whether white ignores atari or tries to run out, black's capture will create two eyes.
White runs out in vain, black captures them creating two eyes.
White ignores the inevitable atari, but white's move doesn't threaten the stones it touches.
White ignores the atari again, trying to take a key point instead. It accomplishes nothing.
This is a mistake for black as it forms bulky five in gote, and not even a connected bulky five.
This is a big mistake for white and allows black to live after all.
This is one of the correct responses for white.
This is one of the correct responses for white.
Black to win in 1 move.
Wrong move. Black formed the twisted four (aka, dogleg four) in gote when white is already occupying one of the two critical points in it. White will take the other critical point and kill black.
White takes the second critical point in twisted four (aka, dogleg four.)
Wrong move. Black is dead now unless white plays the wrong moves. Black has formed bulky five in gote. If white takes the critical point, black dies. If white doesn't take it, black lives.
This is the mistake that would allow black to live.
White's moves beyond this point are inconsequential.
This is the correct move for white, taking the critical point in bulky five. Black is dead now.
Correct move. Black forms one eye and is guaranteed a second eye because white's group cannot escape atari.
White tries to run out in vain, gets captured and black lives.
White ignores black's atari, encroaching on the space. The two white stones still die and black forms his second eye, so black lives.
Black to live in 1 move. Good shape is more important than capturing.
Tempting, but it's a mistake. Black has five seperate groups and would be lucky to form a twisted four (or dogleg four) in gote.
This is a mistake for white that will help black form two eyes.
This is the correct move for white. This is the critical spot that makes it impossible for black to unite its five different groups while still forming two eyes.

White's move here is brutal, putting two of black's stones in atari even though it also places two of white's own stones in atari. Black can only protect the two atari by filling his false eye. Letting them get captured also destroys the false eye.
Black responds more conservatively, dies.
Black thinks he's saved his groups, but he's stretched two thin and will put himself in atari by connecting groups.
Black lives. Black has strengthened his existing groups, formed an eye that he can defend regardless of how white invades, and has room for a second eye.
White has no good moves, this is as good as any.
The idea of sacrificing a second stone to bait black into creating a bad shape doesn't work here.
This is an ambitious move for white, occupying a key point. But as long as black doesn't get greedy and instead reinforces his groups, Black still lives and white just loses more stones.
Black to live in 1 move.

Wrong Move. This looks like the critical point, but the corner point is actually it. This move doesn't put white in atari, isn't a likely eye wall, and is easily isolated from the rest of black's stones.
Wrong move. White is wide open to occupy key spaces until black dies.
Wrong Move. Black seems to form an eye, white influences almost the entire corner, and can take the critical point that finishes off black.
Mistake for white. This is not the critical point in this space, and it only encourages black to strengthen the connections between his groups.
Mistake for white.
White takes the critical point. Black will not be able to form two eyes, white will squeeze black from both sides.
This is the correct move. White will easily capture this stone, but that will give black time to surround it and form a solid second eye.
White doesn't have any good moves here.
White doesn't have any good moves here.
Black to live in 1 move.
This isn't the book's solution, but it still seems unassailable.
Black lives regardless of what move white makes here.
This is a mistake for black, capturing one stone only to lose three stones.
This is black's correct move. White can't recapture because of ko, and the stone in black's territory is dead.
Alternate move, but black still lives.
This is the solution in the book. Black forms an eye and white can't prevent black from bridging the two groups.
Black to live in 1 move.
Wrong move. Black can't form two eyes this way.
Wrong move. At best black can get a square four in gote, which is dead.
This is black's best move, but there's no chance of making life here. At best he has a straight two in gote. And a straight two can't even make life in sente!
Black tries to make more territory, but at best he would get a square four in sente. In reality, he loses these three stones and the other group won't last long.
Wrong move, but a bold move, attempting to carve out enough room for two eyes and make two eyes.
Correct move. Black form a "Big P" in the corner. White can't take both of the critical points at once, so black will live.
Probably white's best move, but it still leaves the other critical point open.
White contains black, but black will still live.
White is still trying to invade black, but black will live and white will just lose more points in the process.
Completely pointless move for white.
This is a decent move from white, taking one of the critical points, but black still takes the other critical point and lives.
Black to live in 1 move
Wrong move. Black forms a bulky five in gote, which is dead.
Even taking this critical point still leaves black with just a bent four in gote with white already occupying one of the critical points.
Crucial mistake for white that accomplishes nothing. Black lives.
Black ignores the critical point. Dies.
Correct move. Black makes one big eye than can either be split into two eyes, or black can get his second eye from white invading from the left.
Mistake, black dies.
Black has two true eyes.
White takes the critical point here, but that gives black time to grab a second eye to the left.
Black to live in 1 move.

Wrong move. Black attempts to form a rabbity six with plenty of room to live. The corner stone will be sacrificed, but black still won't be able to connect the four remaining groups in time to form two eyes.
Black wins whether white makes this aggressive play or not.
This is a mistake for white, missing the chance to put black in double atari.
This is a mistake for black, as white will next put black in triple atari.
White puts black in triple atari. Game over.
White takes a critical point, but black still lives.
Mistake for black.
Black sacrifices the corner stone but forms two eyes and lives.
Black forms two eyes and lives.
White's move here puts black in double atari, removing any chance for making life.
Black still wins.
Wrong move. This forms a bulky five in gote, which is dead.
White takes the critical point of the bulky five, black is dead.
Black is doomed wherever he plays.
This is the critical move that kills black.
Typo, illegal move.
Black is dead.
Wrong move for white.
Black formed two eyes and lives.
Black is still dead.

Wrong move. Black plays conservatively, trying to form a dogleg four which should be alive even in gote. However, the dogleg four in this case isn't connected, it's formed from four distinct groups, so white can kill it without having to take two critical points at once.
White ignored the chance for double atari, taking the wrong critical point in the dogleg four. White dies.
White puts black in double atari, destroying his dogleg four. Black dies.

Correct move. Black plays very conservatively and wins. This move forms one eye and leaves plenty of room for a second no matter how white invades. Black nets four points, either four territory or three territory and one capture.
Black to live in 1.
Wrong move. This seems like a good idea because it forms one good eye, but there is a critical point below white can take to prevent black's second eye.
White denies black a second eye.
Correct move. Black forms one eye and has two possible points to play for the second eye. White can't prevent both points at once.
White plays aggressively, but black still forms the second eye and captures a stone.
White plays more conservatively, but black still gets the second eye and lives.
Wrong move. Black at best forms a bent four, but can't live.
Black to live in 1 move.
Black takes a critical point, pus white's stone in atari, and gains influence on the unconnected black group.
Regardles of where white plays, black gets to form two eyes.
Wrong move. Black forms the bulky five in gote, and dies.
Black lives.
White takes the critical point in the bulky five, black dies.
Wrong move. Black takes a critical point, but without applying the added pressure of putting the white stone in atari.
Wrong move. The best black can do here is form a bent three in gote, which is dead.
Black protects the stone in atari, but forms a bent three in gote, and dies.
Black tries to sacrifice the stone in atari to form one solid eye, but still can't live.
Correct move. It forms one eye, but black can't form the second in time.
Black is on the right track here.
Mistake for white, black lives.
White's correct move, denies black the chance to form a second eye.
Fatal mistake for black. Capturing the stone that was in the eye space lets white take the second corner of the eye. A true eye requires at least three corners.
Alternate move accomplishes nothing.
Black to live in 1 move.
Wrong move. Black forms one eye is smothered.
Mistake for white, black lives.
Corrrect move for white, black dies.
Correct move. White can't prevent black from forming two eyes without putting her own stones in atari, and then black connecting anyway.
Whie has two options here, black lives under both.
Black to live in 1 move.
Corect move. White can't prevent black from forming two eyes now.
Mistake, white can easily invade, denying black the second eye.
Black to live in 1 move.
Correct move. Black formed one solid eye, connected all of its groups, and white can't block the second eye without getting captured in the process, allowing the eye anyway.
White has three options here, all of which end with black living.
Obviousely the wrong move.
Black to live in 1 move.
Wrong move, unless white makes a mistake, black dies.
Black dies if white invades like this.
Mistake for white, black lives.
White captures half of black's stones, the rest fall soon after.
White connects, but that just allows black to connect.
Another bad move for white.
Correct move. Black has two ways to form two eyes now, white can only prevent one of them.
Black to live in 1 move.
Correct move. Black forms one eye and white can't prevent the second eye.
Wrong move, black can't form two eyes now.
This is a mistake for white. Unnecessary, and lets black form two eyes.
White's correct move. Black ends up with a straight two, which is dead.
Black to live in 1 move.
Wrong move. White can easily prevent black from forming two eyes.
Fatal mistake for white, black lives.
Killer move, black dies.
Correct move.
White has three options here, black lives either way.
Black lives.
Black lives.
Black to live in 1 move.
Correct move, black forms one eye and white can't prevent the second eye from forming.
Wrong move. Black doesn't have room to form two eyes because white will take the critical point in the next move.
Black to live in one move.
Correct move. White can't prevent black from forming two eyes here.
White has two options, either way black lives.
White has two options, either way black lives.
Black still lives.
Black still lives.
Wrong move. White can easily prevent black from forming an eye.
This is the correct move for white, black dies.
Mistake for white, black lives.
This is the correct move for white, black dies.
Mistake for white.
Mistake for white.
Black to live in one move.
The book doesn't list this as a solution, but I can't find a way for white to prevent black from forming two eyes here.
Black lives.
Black lives.
Black lives.
Black lives.
Black lives.
Wrong move. Black forms rectangular six in gote, which would be alive if connected, but in the corner, the bottom critical point is easily isolated from the rest.
Mistake for white, black lives.
Typo, illegal move
White's correct move, black dies.
Correct move according to the book. Black can easily connect and form two eyes now.
Fatal mistake for black.
Fatal mistake for black.
This is the correct move for black. It forms one eye and ensures it can form the second.
Either attack from white here fails.
Black to live in one move.
Wrong move for black. Black's three separated groups can't possibly connect and form two eyes in time.
Mistake for white, black lives.
Correct move for white, black dies.
White has two options here, both end in death.
Correct move, black can solidly connect the inner eye, and form a second eye on the edge of the board.
Black to live in one move.
Wrong move for black because of the two groups that are isolated from the rest.
Wrong move for black, forms a dead bent three.
Correct move. Black connects the two weakest groups, leaving plenty of room for them to form an eye with the other two groups, and another eye in the corner.
Weakest move for white, but black lives either way.
Stronger move for white, but black still lives.
This is a mistake, black dies.
This is the move that saves black.
Black to live in one move.
Wrong move, white can outflank black.
Mistake for white, black lives.
Correct move for white, black can't make life now.
Black is dead either way.
Black is dead either way.
Wrong move, white can stop black from making life.
Fatal mistake for white.
Black lives either way.
Black lives either way.
White wins.
Fatal mistake for white.
White wins.
Correct move, black can easily form two eyes.
Black to live in one move.
Another mistake for black. White has too much room to smother black without getting captured first.
Mistake for white, black lives.
Now black is dead.
Promising, but a mistake.
Mistake, white will block black from reaching the edge in time.
Mistake, black can't get any eyes from here.
Correct move. Forms one eye with just enough room to get the second.
Mistake for black.
This is the move that saves black.
Mistake for black.
Mistake for black.
